title: pkcs11: blocking HSM I/O on tokio async executor in get/put/sign
description: secretx-pkcs11/src/lib.rs: get(), put(), and sign() all perform blocking PKCS#11 C calls (open_ro_session, find_objects, get_attributes, create_object, destroy_object, C_Sign) directly on the tokio async executor without spawn_blocking. This blocks a tokio worker thread for the duration of each HSM operation. In a thread-pool executor under concurrent load this causes thread starvation. algorithm() is sync and also blocks when the OnceLock is cold. Fix: wrap HSM calls in tokio::task::spawn_blocking, similar to how secretx-file wraps blocking file I/O. The sign() comment acknowledges the blocking call is intentional but the risk is understated for production loads.
